articles.title: TSX Futures Rise as Bond Yields Fall
articles.expand: Futures linked to the S P/TSX Composite Index traded higher on Monday, as the commodities-heavy benchmark drew support from a combination of falling bond yields and rising resource prices. Yields declined sharply, easing concerns about a pullback in credit activity and bolstering financial stocks. Oil prices extended their gains, underpinning energ...

articles.title: The USDCAD continues the trend higher and reaches a key swing area going back 2025
articles.expand: The USDCAD is pushing higher again today, extending the trend-like rally that began last Monday—and doing so with solid technical backing. Last week’s turning point came when the pair found support near the 100-bar moving average on the 4-hour chart, bottoming at 1.3669 (just above the MA at 1.3662). From there, the buyers took control. The subsequ...

articles.title: Dollar Strengthens After Trump Threat to Kharg Island; Yen Gains on “Final Warning”
articles.expand: Dollar strengthened broadly as markets reacted to a sharp escalation in rhetoric from U.S. President Donald Trump, who warned that Iran’s key energy infrastructure could be “completely obliterated” if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened and a peace deal is not reached “shortly.” In particular, Trump explicitly named Kharg Island—along with Iran’s ...

articles.title: GBP: Repricing risks into spring – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank’s Senior FX Strategist Jane Foley notes that the Pound has been the second best performing G10 currency after the Dollar since the Middle East conflict, driven by a sharp repricing of Bank of England (BoE) policy expectations.

articles.title: Deputies from Iran and the US send mixed messages after Trump's morning pump
articles.expand: The mood is positive in US markets so far this week as Trump talks about a de-escalation. The messages are mixed though and that's leading to some confusion. The oil market remains 1.8% higher but US stock futures are up 0.8% and Treasury yields are down 5-7 bps across the curve. Trump tweeted about negotiations, much as he did last week, but this ...

articles.title: Gold rises as US Treasury yields ease while Middle East tensions escalate
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) regains traction on Monday, reversing earlier intraday losses, as escalating tensions in the Middle East and shifting interest rate expectations keep markets volatile. At the time of writing, XAU/USD is trading around $4,576 after touching a low of $4,419 during the Asian session.

articles.title: Eurozone: Inflation fears rise on war shock – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Chief Economist Bert Colijn highlights that Eurozone sentiment weakened in March as the Middle East war hit confidence. The economic sentiment indicator dropped from 98.3 in February to 96.6, with businesses and consumers more worried about inflation.

articles.title: Brent: War premium drives gains – Danske Bank
articles.expand: Danske Research Team highlights that escalating Middle East tensions and US policy signals are lifting Oil prices, with Brent Crude trading near 115 USD/bbl in early Asian dealings.

articles.title: USD/CAD extends six-day advance as geopolitical tensions bolster US Dollar
articles.expand: USD/CAD trades around 1.3910 on Monday at the time of writing, up 0.12% on the day and extending its winning streak to six consecutive days. The pair is supported by a stronger US Dollar (USD) amid rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

articles.title: USD/JPY: Policymakers eye intervention and BoJ hikes – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Senior Currency Analyst Lee Hardman notes that the Japanese Yen has rebounded, pulling USD/JPY back below 160.00 after verbal warnings from Japan’s currency authorities.

articles.title: S&P 500 bounces from Friday's lows as weekend hedges get unwound. Negotiations in focus.
articles.expand: FUNDAMENTAL OVERVIEW The S P 500 broke through a key support on Friday and extended the losses into new lows as traders hedged into the weekend risk. Those hedges are being unwound today given that nothing happened over the weekend. The focus remains solely on the US-Iran negotiations and there’s some cautious optimism as Pakistan confirmed that ne...

articles.title: Markets Today: Geopolitical premium drives markets, focus shifts to Fed and US jobs report
articles.expand: Middle East conflict drives global market volatility. Asian stocks slide and Euro Area sentiment tumbles on inflation fears. Oil surges toward a record gain, while Gold falls sharply, overshadowed by the rampant US Dollar's rise to near 10-month highs. US labor data and Fed Chair Powell's comments are key next catalysts.

articles.title: CAD: Strength seen moderating on policy repricing – HSBC
articles.expand: HSBC analysts argue the Canadian Dollar (CAD) has outperformed most G10 peers during the Middle East conflict but now appears to be losing momentum.

articles.title: Euro area economic sentiment eases in March as Middle East conflict stokes inflation fears
articles.expand: Euro area economic confidence slipped to 96.6 in March, after recording a revised 98.2 reading in February. That's the lowest estimate since September with consumer confidence (-16.3) also falling back on the month to its lowest since the latter months of 2023. It's all coming undone as the US-Iran war stirs up inflation fears in Europe once again....

articles.title: GBP/JPY Price Analysis: Pound accelerates losses with 210.80 lows eyed
articles.expand: The Pound (GBP) accelerated its downtrend against the Japanese Yen (JPY) on Monday.

articles.title: Canadian Dollar remains subdued due to stronger US Dollar, softer oil prices
articles.expand: USD/CAD continues its winning streak for the sixth successive day, trading around 1.3920 during the European hours on Monday. The pair gains ground as the US Dollar (USD) regains its ground amid increased safe-haven demand on rising doubts over a resolution to the Iran war.

articles.title: Month-end flows point to dollar buying into the fix - BofA
articles.expand: According to BofA's month-end fixing model, the dollar is the one favoured as we look to wrap up March and Q1 trading in the coming days. This fits with what was pointed out by both Credit Agricole and Barclays earlier here . Of note, BofA says that: "Our estimates suggest the potential for (1) material inflows into USD-denominated assets (c.+1.0σ)...

This morning, we are seeing some weakness as those hedges get unwound. articles.title: Swiss KOF Barometer Drops Sharply to 96.1, Signals Broad-Based Economic Weakness
articles.expand: Switzerland’s KOF Economic Barometer fell sharply from 103.8 to 96.1 in March, missing expectations of 100.6 and pointing to a notable deterioration in growth momentum. The decline reflects a broad-based slowdown, with both production and demand-side indicators weakening, suggesting that the economy is losing traction more quickly than anticipated....

articles.title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: US Nonfarm Payrolls and the Middle East war dominating financial boards
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair ends the week around 1.1530, barely below its opening but off the peak at 1.1640. Widespread optimism on Monday was diluted as days went by, and financial markets moved once again on war-related headlines.

articles.expand: Japan has signalled a higher likelihood of FX intervention, with Mimura’s use of “decisive” marking a clear escalation in rhetoric. articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Extra pullbacks should not be ruled out
